preamble: The shift from 'dʲ' to 'ʒ' represents the spirantization of a voiced palatalized dental stop. This evolution typically involves an intermediate voiced palato-alveolar affricate [dʒ] stage.

examples.note: Spirantization of voiced dental stops in various palatalizing contexts, often passing through [dʒ].

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
